item.title: Side Effect
Court Records Should Be Free
item.description: Court records belong to the public. Yet anyone seeking access to federal court filings through PACER, a government software system that stands for Public Access to Court Electronic Records, is usually required to pay hefty fees to search for and view documents. PACER’s fees have long acted as a...

item.title: "Optimizing" concurrent regexes
item.description: I am looking into some regex work, and I ran into a performance problem. I need to run a particular regex over a large number (millions) of strings. That cau...

item.title: Status Is a Distributed System
item.description: Why deployment status is not a UI label, but a consistency problem across databases, queues, workers, locks, reconcilers, Kubernetes, and user trust.

item.description: Hyundai Motor Group is acquiring SoftBank's remaining 9.65% stake in Boston Dynamics for $325 million, completing its full ownership of the robotics

item.title: My agentic engineering workflow (step by step) | Shreyas
item.description: My agentic engineering workflow has changed in the recent past, the models are better, there is much more freedom in choosing the harness, selecting the abilities and actions you could provide. - Pre-planning, pre-idea, pre-everything - The first chat with the agents - The first chat on the terminal - Setting up the Opencode environment - Doing Light research, deep research - Model choices while...
item.author: (Shreyas Prakash)

item.title: AI Engineer Claims to Have Cracked Linear A
item.description: AI Engineer Claims to Have Cracked Linear A

item.title: Self-improving agents still need humans
item.description: Terminal-bench-style scores are tempting progress numbers and very easy to overfit. Used carefully, though, they are a pile of bug reports. goose's Harbor tooling made it cheap to run, pull, compare and explain failures, which led to two small fixes: turn awareness and reading images from disk again.

item.title: How four years at a startup changed the way I work - Diogo Moreira
item.description: Reflections on my time at Amplemarket
item.author: (https://diogomr.com/about)

item.title: I Replaced JSON With a Custom Binary Format. In PHP. :: Tom J.
item.description: We need to store rich text used for web, apps, UIs and editable in editor.Long time ago, it started as pure HTML in DB. When we wanted to split editing and printing, we opted for JSON.But now we want to manipulate data inside effectively. And there, we decided to create a binary format.It proved to be faster, smaller and easier to change.

item.title: How I turned World Cup data into posters
item.description: How I turned World Cup match data into organic, painting-like posters: a breakdown of the data, the chrono-grid, the brush strokes, and the gravity fields behind GenCup 2026.

item.title: The Wrong End of the Problem
item.description: Every company wants AI in their development process right now. That part is clear. What’s less clear is where they’re putting it.

item.title: Project Valhalla, Explained: How a Decade of Work Arrives in JDK 28 - JVM Weekly vol. 180

item.title: IN THE WEIGHTS
item.description: The weights are the billions of numbers forming an AI's brain. Type a name and see how strongly the leading AI models recognize it. Are you in the weights?
